code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multiple failures overriding positives
    - Explicit "don't buy" warnings with defect evidence
    - Conceptually good but fundamentally unusable
    - Negative updates invalidate initial satisfaction

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - "Worse than expected" with demonstrable flaws
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Inferior to alternatives in critical aspects

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with clear compromises
    - Adequate for specific limited-use scenarios
    - Technical competence with notable drawbacks
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction phrasing present
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Factual functionality descriptions without enthusiasm
    - Requires break-in period for full performance
    - Assembly difficulties without functional impact

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise ("Perfect!" "Excellent!")
    - 3+ complementary aspects praised functionally
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Sustained performance validation (>6 months)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH core functional praise
    - Outperforms alternatives in key aspects

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works perfectly" →5 vs "Works fine" →4 (enthusiasm distinction)
    - Praise + functional criticism →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Factual descriptions without emotion →4 ("Fits well")
    - Comparative superiority →5 even with minor drawbacks
    - Solved historical pain points →5 despite initial doubts
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example1:
Review: "UPDATE: Second unit failed faster than first. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override any positives</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed with deep pockets. No complaints."
<Reasoning>Concise enthusiastic praise of core functionality →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example3:
Review: "Sharp well-made tablecloth. Only con is needing to iron cotton."
<Reasoning>Non-functional maintenance vs core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example4:
Review: "Adjustable rod works but requires daily retightening."
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by maintenance →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example5:
Review: "These towels need 2 washes for absorbency but work well after."
<Reasoning>Functional prerequisite impacts initial experience →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example6:
Review: "Between puppy and kids - surprisingly useful long-term!"
<Reasoning>Solves persistent problem exceeding expectations →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example7:
Review: "Material feels cheap but holds items adequately."
<Reasoning>Functional adequacy with quality compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example8:
Review: "Silent operation! Lid sticks sometimes but still 5 stars."
<Reasoning>Non-functional quirk with enthusiastic endorsement →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example9:
Review: "Jewelry hanger concept fails - impractical daily use."
<Reasoning>Core usability failure despite good concept →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example10:
Review: "Fits mattresses adequately. Does the job."
<Reasoning>Factual description lacking enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (29 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 2
  gold 5 -> 3: 1
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is an ok bin. It holds its shape fairly well and is nice and sturdy. There is no side support, so you can't stack something on the top. Color is a nice light pink-nothing too flashy."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 29 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
